title: Key Characteristics of an Effective Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
summary: Outlines the fundamental attributes that make a Work Breakdown Structure clear, complete, and useful for project planning.
content: An effective WBS: (1) Represents all deliverables for the project outcome (product, service, or process); (2) Is hierarchical, with parent‑child relationships that increase granularity; (3) Uses noun phrases only; (4) Follows the 100% rule, capturing 100% of each parent’s scope; (5) Is agreed upon by all stakeholders; (6) May include separate methodology deliverables if desired; (7) Remains agnostic to timing, effort, and cost.
can_answer: What are the essential characteristics of a good Work Breakdown Structure?
How should a WBS be structured to ensure completeness and clarity?
